---
title: "Teaching"
pagetitle: "Teaching — Arvind Karunakaran"
description: "Courses taught by Arvind Karunakaran at Stanford University in Management Science & Engineering and Sociology."
---

I teach courses on organizations, organizational behavior, and the study of
technology and work — at the undergraduate, master's, and doctoral levels.

## Undergraduate & Master's

::: {.stack-list}
- [Spring]{.year}<br>
  **MS&E 180 — Organizations: Theory and Management.** An introduction to
  organization theory and its application to the design and management of
  organizations.
- [Spring]{.year}<br>
  **MS&E 280 — Organizational Behavior: Evidence in Action.** Evidence-based
  approaches to individual and group behavior in organizations.
:::

## Doctoral

::: {.stack-list}
- [Spring]{.year}<br>
  **MS&E 382 / SOC 386 — Meso-Organizational Theory.** A doctoral seminar
  bridging micro and macro perspectives on organizations.
- [Spring]{.year}<br>
  **MS&E 386 / SOC 360 — Doctoral Research Seminar on Technology &
  Organizations.** A research seminar on the study of technology, work, and
  organizations.
- [Autumn · Winter · Spring · Summer]{.year}<br>
  **MS&E 408 — Directed Reading and Research.** Individually supervised reading
  and research.
:::

## Executive & Professional Education

I also teach in executive and professional education programs on organizations,
technological change, and the future of work. Details and materials available
on request.
